careerLevels.name: Startup
careerLevels.title: First Treasury Role
careerLevels.description: A small startup with its first international exposure. One wrong move and the company folds.

flavourText.intro: Welcome to your first treasury role. The founders are counting on you — literally. One bad quarter and the runway runs out.
flavourText.advance: The startup survived! The founders are impressed enough to recommend you for a bigger role.
flavourText.fail: The startup ran out of cash. Your LinkedIn now says 'Open to Work'.

careerLevels.name: Scale-Up
careerLevels.title: Growing Pains
careerLevels.description: More international exposure as the company expands. You're juggling multiple risks now.

flavourText.intro: The company is growing fast. New markets, new currencies, new headaches. Time to prove you can handle complexity.
flavourText.advance: Scale-up complete. You've earned a reputation as someone who can manage growth. A mature business wants you.
flavourText.fail: Too many balls in the air. The board has 'decided to go in a different direction'.

careerLevels.name: Mature Business
careerLevels.title: Comfortable but Sharp
careerLevels.description: Established company with wide margins. More exposures to manage, but less existential pressure.

flavourText.intro: A well-established business with proper governance. The board is experienced but expects professionalism. Don't get complacent.
flavourText.advance: Excellent work at a mature business. But your next challenge will be very different...
flavourText.fail: Steady hand, they said. The board expected better from someone at this level.

careerLevels.name: PE Takeover
careerLevels.title: Under New Management
careerLevels.description: Private equity has acquired the company. Debt has doubled. The new board has zero tolerance for losses.

flavourText.intro: New owners, new rules. The PE partners have loaded the company with debt and your IR exposure has doubled. They want returns, not excuses. Board satisfaction starts at 50 — you're already on thin ice.
flavourText.advance: You survived the PE years. Most don't. A listed company wants someone battle-tested.
flavourText.fail: The PE partners don't do second chances. Your desk has been cleared.

careerLevels.name: Listed Company
careerLevels.title: The Big League
careerLevels.description: Global listed company. Many exposures, analyst scrutiny, and the highest stakes. Treasury Manager of the Year awaits.

flavourText.intro: The pinnacle. A global listed company with exposures across every asset class. Analysts are watching. The board is demanding. Score A+ and you'll be crowned Treasury Manager of the Year.
flavourText.fail: The analysts downgraded the stock. The board needed a scapegoat. Guess who.

aPlusGrade.title: Treasury Manager of the Year
aPlusGrade.description: Your exceptional performance has been recognised by the industry. You've been awarded Treasury Manager of the Year at the annual awards ceremony.
